summary: Iteration 14 — Philippines Quick Access EditModal CRUD — ALL STEPS PASS. Backend regression (3/3 pytest cases, 1.78s): (1) cleanup of any pre-existing PH trip so first-time-create state is achievable; (2) POST /api/travel/trips with country_code='PH' returns flag='🇵🇭', country='Philippines', no leaked _id/user_id; PUT /api/travel/trips/{id} changes destination_name, purpose, departure_date, return_date and persists on GET while preserving country='Philippines'/country_code='PH'; (3) final cleanup leaves DB with zero PH trips. Frontend (Playwright mobile 390x900, logged in as test1@plos.app/test123 → More tab → module-travel): STEP 2 sanity — ph-edit pencil button is visible inside PH pinned card next to PINNED pill; default title 'Philippines Quick Access' rendered when no PH trip exists; default subtitle is 'Manila · Eden Heights Development' (NOT 'Manila & Bulacan · Eden Heights Development' — see note below: code uses phTemplate.destination.city='Manila', so 'Manila & Bulacan' only appears as the final fallback when phTemplate is also null). STEP 3 first-time create — tapping ph-edit opens modal titled 'Set up Philippines Quick Access' with defaults pre-filled (title='Manila & Bulacan', city='Manila', purpose chip 'Eden Heights Development' active). Changed title→'Test PH Trip', purpose→Conference / Training (ph-editor-purpose-conference), departure→'2026-12-15', return→'2026-12-30', tapped ph-editor-save. Modal closed; card header re-rendered to 'Test PH Trip' + subtitle 'Manila · Conference / Training'; an Upcoming Trip card 'trip-b5d22815-…' appeared with 🇵🇭 flag, 'Planning' status pill, dates '2026-12-15 → 2026-12-30', country 'Philippines', '172d to go'. STEP 4 edit existing — tapped ph-edit again, modal title now 'Edit Philippines Quick Access' (matches phEditingId branch); fields pre-filled from previous save (title='Test PH Trip', city='Manila', departure='2026-12-15', return='2026-12-30'); computed border color check confirms only 'conference' chip is active (border rgb(59, 130, 246), all other chips transparent). Changed title→'Manila & Bulacan', purpose→Eden Heights Development, saved. Card now reads 'Manila & Bulacan' + 'Manila · Eden Heights Development'. STEP 5 tap-through still works — tapping ph-pinned (card body, NOT pencil) navigated to /travel/b5d22815-b48c-449b-8d6b-3495076a3cab; trip-hero showed '🇵🇭 / Manila & Bulacan / Manila · Philippines / 2026-12-15 → 2026-12-30 / 172d to go / UTC+8 (13h ahead of Atlanta EST, 12h ahead during EDT)' — confirming the persisted dates/city carry into the trip planner. STEP 6 pencil doesn't trigger card tap — opened editor via pencil, URL remained /travel during modal-open, closed via ph-editor-close (X), URL stayed /travel — stopPropagation working correctly.

issues: Spec said default subtitle (no PH trip) would be 'Manila & Bulacan · Eden Heights Development', but actual is 'Manila · Eden Heights Development'. Cause: code uses `phTrip?.city || phTemplate?.destination?.city || 'Manila & Bulacan'`, and the template returns city='Manila', so 'Manila & Bulacan' only renders if BOTH phTrip and phTemplate are null. Either the spec wording is slightly off, or the intent was to use phTemplate.destination_name ('Manila & Bulacan') for the subtitle city when no trip exists. App is internally consistent — no functional bug — but main agent should confirm desired default copy.

react_native_issues.issue: Two console warnings persist: 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' (sourced from a dependency, not app code). Cosmetic, carry-over from iters 11/12/13. Reference: console output, no file change required in app.

action_items: (Optional clarification) Decide whether the default PH card subtitle (no trip yet) should read 'Manila & Bulacan · Eden Heights Development' (spec) or 'Manila · Eden Heights Development' (current). If the former, swap `phTemplate?.destination?.city` for `phTemplate?.destination?.destination_name` in the `phSubtitleCity` fallback at travel/index.tsx:135.

(Carry-over from iter 10–13) Split /app/backend/server.py into per-module routers and extract seeds (TRAVEL_ADVISORIES, ATL_MNL_FLIGHTS, MANILA_HOTELS, PH_CACHED_INSIGHTS) into /app/backend/seed/travel.py.
critical_code_review_comments: travel/index.tsx:195-206 — openPhEdit correctly seeds phEditorInitial from phTrip when present, falling back to phTemplate, then to hard-coded defaults. Editing-vs-creating is tracked by `phEditingId` (trip_id or null) which drives both the modal title and the PUT vs POST branch in submitPhEdit. Clean dual-mode pattern.

travel/index.tsx:208-237 — submitPhEdit hard-codes country='Philippines'/country_code='PH' on every save, so users cannot accidentally change the country via the PH editor. On create path, also pre-caches PH insights via POST /api/travel/insights (fast hardcoded path, <100ms) so opening the planner is instant. Insight caching errors are swallowed (try/catch) so trip creation never fails on transient insight errors — good defensive coding.

travel/index.tsx:321-329 — The pencil TouchableOpacity uses inline `onPress={(e) => { (e as any)?.stopPropagation?.(); openPhEdit(); }}`. On react-native-web this propagates via the DOM event correctly (verified — STEP 6 URL did not change). On native React Native, TouchableOpacity inside another TouchableOpacity uses gesture responder chain rather than DOM bubbling — the parent typically does NOT fire when child handles the press, so this works there too. hitSlop=8 ensures the 28x28 button has an effective 44+ tap target.

EditModal.tsx:91-97 — `update` merges into prior state and supports a cascade callback. The editor correctly re-seeds `values` from `initial` whenever `visible` flips to true (lines 83-89), so reopening with a different phTrip refreshes the fields. Verified in STEP 4 — fields pre-fill from the freshly created trip on second open.

EditModal.tsx:112-115 — text fields convert '' to null on submit. submitPhEdit re-applies trim-or-default fallbacks ('Manila & Bulacan'/'Manila') so an accidentally cleared title still produces a valid trip. Solid defensive layer.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_14_ph_edit.py (NEW — 3 pytest cases: cleanup, PUT-preserves-country-and-country-code with Create→PUT→GET verification + create-response assertions, final cleanup)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (steps 2–6 verified: ph-edit visible & next to PINNED, first-time create with 'Set up' modal title and prefilled defaults, save → card title+subtitle update + new Upcoming Trip with 🇵🇭 + Planning + dates, edit modal title='Edit Philippines Quick Access' with prefilled values + correct active purpose chip, save → revert to 'Manila & Bulacan' + 'Manila · Eden Heights Development', ph-pinned tap-through → /travel/{trip_id} with hero showing all persisted values, pencil stopPropagation verified — URL stays /travel through open+close)

seed_data_creation: Backend regression cleans up any pre-existing PH trip at module start (so UI test always starts in first-time-create state), creates one TEST_PH_Backend trip and deletes it at teardown. Frontend test creates one real PH trip (named 'Test PH Trip' then edited to 'Manila & Bulacan' with departure 2026-12-15 / return 2026-12-30 / purpose eden_heights) — left in DB for main agent inspection; can be deleted via DELETE /api/travel/trips/b5d22815-b48c-449b-8d6b-3495076a3cab if not needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 14 verified: PH pinned card pencil EditModal CRUD works end-to-end. Patterns reused from iter 13: (1) Login via [data-testid='login-email-input']/[data-testid='login-password-input']/[data-testid='login-submit-button']. (2) Tabs lack testIDs — use `page.get_by_text('More', exact=True).first.click(force=True)` then `await (await page.query_selector('[data-testid="module-travel"]')).evaluate('el => el.click()')`. (3) EditModal testIDs are `${testID}-${field_key}` for inputs, `${testID}-${field_key}-${option_value}` for select chips, `${testID}-save`, `${testID}-close`. For PH editor: ph-editor-destination_name, ph-editor-city, ph-editor-departure_date, ph-editor-return_date, ph-editor-purpose-{business|leisure|eden_heights|family|conference|medical|mixed}, ph-editor-status-{planning|booked|completed}, ph-editor-save, ph-editor-close. (4) The modal title is the only differentiator between create ('Set up Philippines Quick Access') and edit ('Edit Philippines Quick Access') modes — both use the same testID prefix. (5) Default PH subtitle when no trip+phTemplate exists is 'Manila · Eden Heights Development' (template city=Manila wins over hard-coded fallback 'Manila & Bulacan'). (6) After save, a single load() refresh re-fetches trips list — Upcoming Trips section repopulates within ~2-3s. (7) Backend PUT /api/travel/trips/{id} returns 200 with full updated trip; GET /api/travel/trips includes it in 'trips' array with computed flag/days_until_departure. No _id/user_id leak observed.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects detected. Only a minor wording divergence between spec ('Manila & Bulacan · Eden Heights Development' default subtitle) and implementation ('Manila · Eden Heights Development'). The implementation is internally consistent and uses the template's actual city; flagged in action_items for main agent to confirm intent.
